chunk_summary: Guidelines for evaluating childhood obesity in India, including thresholds for comorbidities, referral criteria, and follow-up plans based on IAP and WHO standards.
learning_objectives: Understand obesity severity classification using IAP and WHO thresholds for Indian children.
Learn criteria for endocrine and genetic evaluations in pediatric obesity cases.
Interpret lab results for dysglycemia, MASLD/NAFLD risk, and hypertension in obese children.
Develop structured follow-up plans based on comorbidity-driven lab findings.
key_facts: Obesity thresholds: BMI-for-age cutoffs of 23 for overweight risk and 27 for obesity in Asian Indian children (IAP).
Dysglycemia: Prediabetes fasting glucose 100–125 mg/dL; diabetes fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL.
MASLD/NAFLD risk: Elevated ALT >80 U/L warrants expedited assessment; normal ALT values are <26 U/L (males) and <22 U/L (females).
Endocrine evaluation: Indicated for obesity with short stature or falling height velocity.
Genetic evaluation: Lower threshold for obesity onset before 5 years, hyperphagia, consanguinity, dysmorphism, or developmental delay.
reference_quote: Use IAP thresholds to decide urgency and referral. Classify severity using the chart (under-5 WHO weight-for-height z-scores; 5–18 years IAP BMI-for-age with adult-equivalent cutoffs 23 for overweight risk and 27 for obesity in Asian Indian children).
document_key_topics: Obesity severity classification in Indian children
Comorbidity thresholds for dysglycemia and MASLD/NAFLD
Criteria for endocrine and genetic evaluations
Structured follow-up plans for abnormal lab findings
